mission.title: Install and Configure Velero (Backup & Restore Operator)
mission.description: Velero is a backup and restore operator for Kubernetes, allowing users to back up cluster resources and persistent volumes, and migrate them across clusters. It is particularly useful for disaster recovery and development purposes. Key trade-offs include the need for proper configuration and potential complexity in managing backups and restores.

steps.title: Add the Velero Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Velero Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vmware-tanzu https://vmware-tanzu.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This repository contains the Velero Helm charts.

steps.title: Install Velero with Helm
steps.description:
This command installs Velero in the 'velero' namespace with the specified version.

```bash

helm install velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --create-namespace --version 1.18.0

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions to create namespaces.

steps.title: Create a Backup Storage Location
steps.description:
This step configures a backup storage location for Velero, which is necessary for storing backups.

```bash

velero install --provider aws --bucket <YOUR_BUCKET_NAME> --secret-file <YOUR_AWS_SECRET_FILE> --region <YOUR_AWS_REGION>

```

> Replace placeholders with your actual AWS bucket and credentials.

steps.title: Verify Velero Installation
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Velero deployment to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> You should see the Velero server pod running.

steps.title: Create a Backup
steps.description:
This command creates a backup of your Kubernetes resources.

```bash

velero backup create my-backup --include-namespaces <YOUR_NAMESPACE>

```

> Replace <YOUR_NAMESPACE> with the namespace you want to back up.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Velero
uninstall.description:
This step removes Velero and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all backups stored in the configured storage location.

```bash

helm uninstall velero --namespace velero

kubectl delete namespace velero

```

> This command will remove the Velero release.
> This command will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Velero
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration. This process includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading, and verifying the installation.

```bash

velero backup get

helm upgrade velero vmware-tanzu/velero --namespace velero --version 1.18.0

kubectl get pods -n velero

```

> Check existing backups before proceeding.
> This command upgrades Velero to the specified version.
> Verify that the Velero pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create backup'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the backup storage location is misconfigured or inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check your storage configuration and ensure the bucket is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Velero server is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Velero server pod may have failed to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n velero' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No backups found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that no backups have been created yet.

**Fix:**
Create a backup using 'velero backup create <backup-name>'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the service account used by Velero lacks necessary permissions.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service account has the correct roles and permissions assigned.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new backup storage providers and improved restore performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy backup storage configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.17, review the new storage provider configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see the Velero server pod running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n velero'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary tools installed.
